content:
Your resume is well-structured, with a clear format that makes it easy to read. The skills section is strong, but your work experience section could be improved by adding more detailed bullet points that show specific accomplishments or contributions in each role. Employers want to see results, so including quantifiable details will make your resume stand out even more.

With a few small refinements, your materials will be even stronger. Keep up the great work!

Best,
Professor Padavano

Grades:
• Cover Letter: 82/100
• Resume: 86/100

⸻

Letter for Duaa Eltahir

Dear Duaa,

Thank you for submitting your resume and cover letter. You’ve put in great effort, and your materials showcase your strengths well. Below is my feedback.

Your cover letter is strong in structure and professionalism. You clearly communicate your interest in the role and your relevant skills. One area for improvement would be the introduction—it could be more engaging to immediately capture the reader’s attention. Additionally, while your letter is well-organized, adding more specifics about the company and why you’re a great fit would strengthen it. Right now, the letter is solid, but it could feel more personalized.
context: From document: Feedback Letters for Resume & Cover Letters — chunk 14 (chunk 14/22)
